text.title: Writing Specifications (SDD core) Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Writing Specifications (SDD core). Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: spec.md answers WHAT + WHY; HOW lives in design.md.
rule.rationale: spec.md answers WHAT + WHY; HOW lives in design.md.

rule.statement: Every FR is SMART; non-SMART rows are rejected.
rule.rationale: Every FR is SMART; non-SMART rows are rejected.

rule.statement: Every FR has ≥1 Given-When-Then AC with concrete values.
rule.rationale: Every FR has ≥1 Given-When-Then AC with concrete values.

rule.statement: FR-X / NFR-X / AC-X / US-X ids are stable across versions; renumbering is forbidden.
rule.rationale: FR-X / NFR-X / AC-X / US-X ids are stable across versions; renumbering is forbidden.

rule.statement: spec.md carries an explicit 'Out of scope' section listing contentious items.
rule.rationale: spec.md carries an explicit 'Out of scope' section listing contentious items.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-writing-specifications.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
